commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - The remaining blocker is maintainer security/product judgment on reusing allowSymlinkTargets for writes, not an automation-repairable defect.; Needs attention: The diff changes a security-sensitive filesystem write boundary by reusing a load-time symlink trust list for apply-time writes. (sha=57bf1c6390ed7cb7e16c797e6225dae3e55dadb3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads runtime config into Skill Workshop apply entrypoints, shares the skill symlink-target resolver, and writes apply targets through configured trusted symlink roots with tests and docs.

PR surface: Source +117, Tests +69, Docs +2. Total +188 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, though I did not rerun it locally. Current main discovers configured symlinked workspace skills but apply lacks the config-backed write allowance; the PR body supplies terminal proof for the fixed trusted and untrusted paths.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining blocker is maintainer security/product judgment on reusing `allowSymlinkTargets` for writes, not an automation-repairable defect.

target.title: Allow Skill Workshop apply through trusted skill symlinks
target.body:
## Summary
- Reuse `skills.load.allowSymlinkTargets` for Skill Workshop apply-time writes through symlinked workspace skill paths.
- Thread active config into CLI, gateway, and agent-tool apply paths.
- Preflight the target before publishing support files, and keep untrusted symlink targets blocked.
- Add regression coverage and docs for trusted/untrusted Workshop symlink behavior.

## Tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts src/skills/loading/workspace-load.test.ts src/skills/runtime/refresh.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/skill-workshop-tool.ts src/cli/skills-cli.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.ts src/skills/loading/workspace.ts src/skills/loading/symlink-targets.ts src/skills/runtime/refresh.ts src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts src/skills/workshop/service.ts src/skills/workshop/store.ts src/skills/workshop/types.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/tools/skill-workshop-tool.ts src/cli/skills-cli.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.ts src/skills/loading/workspace.ts src/skills/loading/symlink-targets.ts src/skills/runtime/refresh.ts src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts src/skills/workshop/service.ts src/skills/workshop/store.ts src/skills/workshop/types.ts`
- `$HOME/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Tooling notes
- `pnpm check:changed` did not run checks because local Crabbox is `0.19.0`, while the Testbox provider requires Crabbox `>=0.22.0`.
- Repo-wide `pnpm format:check` reports pre-existing unrelated formatting issues; the touched-file format check above passes.
- A post-format repo-wide `pnpm lint:core` rerun hit a `tsgolint` Go `SIGSEGV` after the `src:skills` shard passed; direct `oxlint` on touched files passes.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Skill Workshop apply can now write an update through a workspace `skills` symlink only when the symlink target is configured in `skills.load.allowSymlinkTargets`; an untrusted symlink target remains blocked before writing.
- Real environment tested: macOS filesystem workspace using the PR-head CLI `OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (57bf1c6)`, isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and isolated `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`. The workspace had `<proof-root>/workspace/skills -> <proof-root>/shared-skills`, and the config set `agents.defaults.workspace` to `<proof-root>/workspace` plus `skills.load.allowSymlinkTargets: ["<proof-root>/shared-skills"]`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Created a real temporary OpenClaw state directory, workspace directory, shared skills directory, and filesystem symlink `workspace/skills -> shared-skills`.
  2. Wrote a real workspace skill at `<proof-root>/shared-skills/shared-skill/SKILL.md` and a real proposal draft at `<proof-root>/draft/PROPOSAL.md`.
  3. Ran `node scripts/run-node.mjs config validate --json` with `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<proof-root>/state` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<proof-root>/openclaw.json`.
  4. Ran `node scripts/run-node.mjs skills workshop propose-update shared-skill --proposal <proof-root>/draft/PROPOSAL.md --json` with the same isolated environment.
  5. Ran `node scripts/run-node.mjs skills workshop apply shared-skill-20260608-ce635dbad9 --json` with the same isolated environment.
  6. Verified the marker in the symlink target file with `grep -n trusted-symlink-proof-20260608 <proof-root>/shared-skills/shared-skill/SKILL.md` and verified the symlink target with `readlink <proof-root>/workspace/skills`.
  7. Repeated the apply path against a second isolated workspace with `workspace/skills -> external-skills` and no `skills.load.allowSymlinkTargets` entry to confirm the write guard still blocks untrusted targets.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
```text
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (57bf1c6)

$ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<proof-root>/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<proof-root>/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config validate --json
{"valid":true,"path":"<proof-root>/openclaw.json"}

$ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<proof-root>/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<proof-root>/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs skills workshop propose-update shared-skill --proposal <proof-root>/draft/PROPOSAL.md --json
record.id: shared-skill-20260608-ce635dbad9
record.status: pending
record.target.skillFile: <proof-root>/workspace/skills/shared-skill/SKILL.md
record.scan.state: clean

$ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<proof-root>/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<proof-root>/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs skills workshop apply shared-skill-20260608-ce635dbad9 --json
record.status: applied
targetSkillFile: <proof-root>/workspace/skills/shared-skill/SKILL.md

$ grep -n trusted-symlink-proof-20260608 <proof-root>/shared-skills/shared-skill/SKILL.md
8:After apply marker: trusted-symlink-proof-20260608

$ readlink <proof-root>/workspace/skills
<proof-root>/shared-skills

$ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<untrusted-proof-root>/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<untrusted-proof-root>/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs skills workshop apply untrusted-symlink-skill-20260608-70cabdd258 --json
exit=1
Error: Skill file resolves through an untrusted symlink target: <untrusted-proof-root>/workspace/skills/untrusted-symlink-skill/SKILL.md. Configure skills.load.allowSymlinkTargets for intentional skill symlinks.

$ test -e <untrusted-proof-root>/external-skills/untrusted-symlink-skill/SKILL.md || echo "target not written"
target not written
```
- Observed result after fix: The trusted symlinked update proposal applied successfully and wrote the new marker into the real shared skills target, while the untrusted symlinked create proposal exited nonzero and did not create the target skill file.
- What was not tested: Live gateway delivery, Discord delivery, and a live agent-tool turn were not exercised in this proof run; the real CLI path and shared `applySkillProposal` filesystem write boundary were exercised directly.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof used isolated local OpenClaw state/config instead of the contributor machine live gateway so it would not mutate an operator fleet.
